〈雪菜〉：お二人の仲が良くて。: Yukina: “That you two get along so well.”
〈雪菜〉：歳の離れたお友達って、何だか憧れちゃいます。: Yukina: “Friends with an age gap... I kind of admire that.”
〈咲美〉：なら、石原さんもなっちゃえば？: Saki: “Then, why don't you become one too, Ishihara-san?”
〈咲美〉：そんな事はないと思うけどなぁ…: Saki: “I don't think that’s true at all, though...”
〈彦麻呂〉：だ、だから慣れてないんですよ。: Hikomaro: “I—I told you, I am not used to this.”
〈咲美〉：せっかくのチャンスなのに…。: Saki: “And it’s such a perfect chance...”
〈咲美〉：みんなでオトモダチになるチャンス。: Saki: “A chance for us all to be friends.”
〈咲美〉：仕事も歳もバラバラで、面白いと思う。: Saki: “I think it’d be interesting since our jobs and ages are all different.”
〈咲美〉：じゃ、初めの一歩の計画を立てよ。石原さんはお休みの日とか…。: Saki: “Then, let’s make a plan for the first step. Ishihara-san, what about your days off...?”
〈雪菜〉：ウチのお店は、年中無休ですから…。: Yukina: “Our shop is open year-round, so...”
〈雪菜〉：この界隈はラーメン屋さんの激戦区です。: Yukina: “This area is a highly competitive zone for ramen shops.”
〈雪菜〉：綾瀬さんは、普通に土日が休みなんですか？: Yukina: “Ayase-san, are you normally off on Saturdays and Sundays?”
〈咲美〉：基本的には。雨がずーっと降り続いて、スケジュールが遅れた時は、３週間ぐ: Saki: “Basically. When it keeps raining and the schedule gets delayed, I might work for three weeks straight, though.”
〈雪菜〉：あの、もしかして綾瀬さんのお仕事は…。: Yukina: “Um, by any chance, Ayase-san, your job is...”
〈咲美〉：見てのとおり建設現場で働いてる。今はみんなで力を合わせて、でっかい、ホ: Saki: “As you can see, I work at construction sites. Right now, we’re all working together to build a huge home center.”
〈雪菜〉：太雪亭へ来て頂いた時は、セーター姿でしたから、ちっとも知りませんでした: Yukina: “When you came to Taisetsutei, you were wearing a sweater, so I didn't know at all.”
〈咲美〉：たまに言葉が汚くなるのは許してね。悪気とかはぜんっぜんないし、親しくな: Saki: “Please forgive me if my language gets a bit foul sometimes. I don't mean anything by it at all; it’s just the closer I get to someone, the more it happens.”
〈雪菜〉：あの、私、綾瀬さんの喋り方、好きです。: Yukina: “Um, I... I like the way you talk, Ayase-san.”
〈雪菜〉：お世辞じゃなく、本当に。: Yukina: “Not just to flatter you, really.”
〈咲美〉：っ、何だか照れるんだけど…: Saki: “Kh... I’m actually a bit embarrassed...”
〈咲美〉：…完全に置物化してる。: Saki: “...He’s turned completely into an ornament.”
〈咲美〉：じゃ、褒められたところで、気分良く帰ろっかな。: Saki: “Well, now that I’ve been complimented, I’ll head home while I’m in a good mood.”
〈雪菜〉：なら私も一緒に。あまり長居すると、先生に悪いですし。: Yukina: “Then I’ll go too. It would be bad of me to stay too long and bother Sensei.”
〈咲美〉：ちっとも悪くないと思うよ。: Saki: “I don't think it’s a bother at all.”
〈咲美〉：センセーと少しも話をしてないし。: Saki: “You haven't talked to Sensei much at all.”
〈雪菜〉：さすがに二人きりというのは、ちょっと…。: Yukina: “As expected, being alone with him is a bit...”
〈雪菜〉：緊張しちゃって、たぶん、何も話せません。: Yukina: “I’d get nervous and probably wouldn't be able to say anything.”
〈咲美〉：勝手な事を言った私が馬鹿でした。: Saki: “I was foolish for saying whatever I pleased.”
〈咲美〉：私の事は別にして、ちゃんと女の人を見る目が、あるんだなって。: Saki: “Leaving me aside, you really do have an eye for women.”
〈咲美〉：変な事を言ってゴメン。じゃ、私、本当に帰らないと。: Saki: “Sorry for saying something weird. Well, I really have to go.”
〈彦麻呂〉：…これからお買い物して、夕御飯の支度ですよね？: Hikomaro: “...Are you going to go shopping and prepare dinner now?”
〈彦麻呂〉：頂いたギョーザ、奥さんと半分コしても、いいですかぁ？: Hikomaro: “Would it be alright if I shared the gyoza I received with the Madam?”
〈彦麻呂〉：せっかくですから旦那さんにも。: Hikomaro: “Since it's a special occasion, your husband should have some too.”
〈咲美〉：マー君も喜ぶと思う。: Saki: “I’m sure Ma-kun will be happy.”
〈彦麻呂〉：これをキッカケに、是非、太雪亭にも足を…。: Hikomaro: “Please take this opportunity to visit Taisetsutei by all means...”
〈雪菜〉：っ、何だかすみません。: Yukina: “Kh... I’m sorry for some reason.”
〈咲美〉：少しでも役に立てれば私も嬉しいし。太雪亭はギョーザも美味しいから、きっ: Saki: “I’m happy if I can be of even a little help. The gyoza at Taisetsutei are delicious, so I’m sure he’ll spread the word.”
〈彦麻呂〉：それは良いアイデアですねぇ。: Hikomaro: “That is a wonderful idea-de ojaru ne.”
〈咲美〉：マー君は事務職の知り合いが多いし。: Saki: “Ma-kun knows a lot of people in office jobs.”
〈咲美〉：私、もしかして出過ぎた事を言った？: Saki: “Did I say something out of line?”
〈雪菜〉：ご夫婦の、仲睦まじさを感じちゃいました。: Yukina: “I just felt how close you are as a married couple.”
〈咲美〉：べ、別にノロケたつもりはないけど…。: Saki: “I-I didn't mean to brag or anything...”
奥さんの指輪をじっと見つめてた。: She was staring intently at the Madam’s ring.
〈咲美〉：人生という道を、二人で歩いて…何があってもずっと一緒で。: Saki: “Walking the path of life together... being together forever no matter what happens.”
〈雪菜〉：そういうの、憧れちゃいます。: Yukina: “I admire that kind of thing.”
〈咲美〉：ちなみにセンセーは独身だから。バツ無し子無しのお医者さん。: Saki: “By the way, Sensei is single. A doctor with no ex-wife and no kids.”
〈咲美〉：ギョーザありがと。じゃ、本当にもう行く。: Saki: “Thanks for the gyoza. Well, I’m really going now.”
〈雪菜〉：兄の事は本当にすみません。: Yukina: “I’m truly sorry about my brother.”
〈彦麻呂〉：もう謝らないで下さいな。サイフを忘れたのは私ですし、誤解を受けやすい: Hikomaro: “Please do not apologize anymore. I am the one who forgot my wallet, and I have an appearance that is easily misunderstood.”
〈雪菜〉：お話ができて本当に良かったです。: Yukina: “I’m really glad I could talk to you.”
〈咲美〉：…住んでる場所って、太雪亭と同じ？: Saki: “...Do you live in the same place as Taisetsutei?”
〈雪菜〉：お店の二階に、兄と二人で。: Yukina: “On the second floor of the shop, with just my brother.”
〈咲美〉：けっこう距離があるよね。途中まで一緒だし、私、送って行くよ。: Saki: “It’s quite a distance. It’s on my way, so I’ll walk you part of the way.”
〈雪菜〉：っ、一人で大丈夫です。: Yukina: “Kh... I’m fine on my own.”
〈咲美〉：だーめ。アーケード抜けた後、一気に道が暗くなるし。: Saki: “Nope. The road gets dark all at once after you pass the arcade.”
〈雪菜〉：っ、何だか迷惑をかける為に来たみたいで…。: Yukina: “Kh... I feel like I just came here to be a nuisance...”
〈咲美〉：先に玄関を出て待ってて。私、窓から脱出しないと。: Saki: “Go wait outside the front door first. I have to make my escape through the window.”
〈咲美〉：脱いだ靴が軒の上に置いてある。: Saki: “My shoes are placed on top of the eaves.”
〈咲美〉：理由は歩きながら教えてあげるね。: Saki: “I’ll tell you the reason while we walk.”
〈咲美〉：ギョーザ、分けて貰って、サンキュね。: Saki: “Thanks for letting me have some gyoza.”
〈彦麻呂〉：そりゃあ痴漢とか暴漢とか。: Hikomaro: “Well, gropers or muggers and the like.”
〈彦麻呂〉：奥さんは、自分を客観的に見れておりません。: Hikomaro: “Madam, you are unable to view yourself objectively-desuuuu.”
〈彦麻呂〉：腕っ節が男勝りなのは認めますが…。: Hikomaro: “I admit your physical strength rivals that of a man, but...”
〈咲美〉：女の私から見ても、彼女、良い感じだねぇ。: Saki: “Even from a woman’s perspective, she seems like a good catch.”
〈咲美〉：容姿端麗で清潔感があって、何より性格がお嫁さん向き。身持ちの堅そうなと: Saki: “She’s beautiful, has a clean feel about her, and above all, her personality is perfect for a wife. The fact that she seems modest is especially a plus.”
〈咲美〉：だからこそ悪ふざけは無しだ。: Saki: “Which is exactly why no practical jokes are allowed.”
〈咲美〉：真面目なお付き合いならいいけど、また医者という立場を利用して、エッチな: Saki: “It’s fine if it’s a serious relationship, but you can’t use your position as a doctor to do perverted things.”
〈彦麻呂〉：逸脱した診察行為は致しません。: Hikomaro: “I shall not engage in any medical acts that deviate from the norm.”
〈咲美〉：あんな子を傷つけたら、バチが当たるからね。: Saki: “If you hurt a girl like that, you’ll be punished by heaven.”
〈彦麻呂〉：そのバチを当てるのは…。: Hikomaro: “And the one to deliver that punishment is...”
〈彦麻呂〉：肝に銘じておきますぅ。: Hikomaro: “I shall keep that engraved in my heart-desuuuu.”
〈咲美〉：なら、明日から通っちゃおう。: Saki: “Well then, start going there from tomorrow.”
〈咲美〉：恋愛はマメなのが鉄則だろ？ 毎日ちゃんと太雪亭へ通って、お店のお馴染み: Saki: “Isn't it a rule that romance requires diligence? Go to Taisetsutei properly every day, become a regular, and get along with the brother too.”
〈彦麻呂〉：…できるだけ努力しますが。: Hikomaro: “...I shall make as much effort as possible.”
〈咲美〉：じゃ、次は日曜日に。おやすみ、センセー。: Saki: “Well then, next is Sunday. Goodnight, Sensei.”
メソメソしてた時より、ずっと元気になってた。: He felt much more energetic than when he was blubbering.
明日から通うでおじゃるよ。: I shall start going there from tomorrow-de ojaru yooo.
奥さんの後押しに応える為。: To respond to the Madam’s support.
そして何より自分自身の幸せの為。: And above all, for my own happiness.
麻呂は本格的に婚活を開始するでおじゃるぅ。: Maro shall begin his marriage hunt in earnest-de ojaru yoooo.
〈彦麻呂〉：いつの日かこのギョーザを、ヨメになった雪菜ちゃんと一緒に、ハフハフ食: Hikomaro: “Someday, I want to eat these gyoza together with Yukina-chan as my wife, huffing and puffing on them-de ojaru ne, hohoho!”
…そして翌日のお昼時…: ...And then, during lunchtime the next day...
〈彦麻呂〉：ちゃんとサイフは持ってきてますよぉ。: Hikomaro: “I have brought my wallet properly today-desuuuu.”
〈彦麻呂〉：今日は塩ラーメンと半チャーハンでしたねぇ。メニューが豊富なので、選び: Hikomaro: “Today it was the salt ramen and half-fried rice-de ojaru ne. The menu is so plentiful, it is worth the effort of choosing... hoho.”
〈雪菜〉：…お味のほうは、お口に？: Yukina: “...Was the flavor to your liking?”
〈彦麻呂〉：いいんですよ…それではまた。: Hikomaro: “It is quite alright... until next time.”
…そしてまた翌日のお昼時…: ...And then, during lunchtime the following day...
〈彦麻呂〉：今日は担々麺に野菜炒めを頂きましたぁ。ライスをつけようか迷いましたが: Hikomaro: “Today I had the tantanmen and the stir-fried vegetables-desuuuu. I debated whether to add rice, and I find myself slightly regretting that I did not... hoho.”
〈雪菜〉：野菜炒めは麺類以外で、ウチのお薦めなんです。: Yukina: “The stir-fried vegetables are our top recommendation aside from the noodles.”
〈彦麻呂〉：そうじゃないかと思いましたぁ。: Hikomaro: “I thought that might be the case-desuuuu.”
〈雪菜〉：本当に、無口ですみません。: Yukina: “Really, I’m sorry he’s so silent.”
…そしてまたまた翌日のお昼時…: ...And once again, during lunchtime the day after that...
〈雪菜〉：いつもいつも、すみません。: Yukina: “I’m sorry for always... every time.”
〈雪菜〉：い、いつもいつもすみません。: Yukina: “I-I’m sorry for every single time.”
〈彦麻呂〉：今日はタンメン大盛りにライスでしたぁ。やはり麺類はアツアツのうち頂く: Hikomaro: “Today was the large portion of tanmen and rice-desuuuu. As I thought, the rule for eating noodles more deliciously is to have them while they are piping hot-de ojaru ne.”
〈雪菜〉：…お味のほうはお口に？: Yukina: “...Was the flavor to your liking?”
〈雪菜〉：っ、確かにそうかも知れないです。: Yukina: “Kh... that may indeed be true.”
〈彦麻呂〉：昨日に引き続き、遠慮なくどうぞ。: Hikomaro: “Just like yesterday, do not hesitate-desuuuu.”
〈雪菜〉：っ、お味のほうは、お口に？: Yukina: “Kh... was the flavor to your liking?”
〈彦麻呂〉：同意して頂いて光栄ですぅ。: Hikomaro: “I am honored that you agree-desuuuu.”
〈雪菜〉：じゃあ今日も、お味のほうは…。: Yukina: “Then today as well, as for the taste...”
〈雪菜〉：お兄ちゃん、今度こそ、聞こえた？: Yukina: “Onii-chan, for sure this time, did you hear?”
〈彦麻呂〉：反応するのは文句の時だけですかぁ。: Hikomaro: “So you only react when there is a complaint-de ojaru ka.”
〈兄貴〉：ウチの料理のどこがマズいんだ？: Aniki: “What part of our food is bad?”
〈彦麻呂〉：誰も料理とは言っておりません。: Hikomaro: “I never said it was the food-desuuuu.”
〈兄貴〉：ビビッていきなり掌返しかよ。誤魔化そうとしても、そーはいかないぜ。: Aniki: “You're scared and suddenly backtracking? Even if you try to dodge, it won't work.”
〈彦麻呂〉：私に対してはどーでも良いですが、カウンターにいたお客さんへの、接客態: Hikomaro: “I do not care what you think of me, but your customer service toward the person at the counter was the absolute worst-desuuuu.”
〈彦麻呂〉：アレコレ話しかけてるのにガン無視。さすがにあの態度は、マズいと思いま: Hikomaro: “They were talking to you about this and that, and you completely ignored them. I think that attitude is ‘bad’ indeed-desuuuu.”
〈兄貴〉：ウチはメシを喰わせる場所であって、お話をしに来る場所じゃねーんだよ。: Aniki: “This is a place to feed people, not a place to come and chat.”
〈彦麻呂〉：客に合わせるのも商売のウチでは？: Hikomaro: “Isn’t matching the customer's pace also part of the business-de ojaru ka?”
〈兄貴〉：そんなの味に自信のない店がやるこった。: Aniki: “That’s what shops with no confidence in their taste do.”
〈彦麻呂〉：たぶん二度と来ませんねぇ、あの人。: Hikomaro: “That person likely will never return-de ojaru ne.”
〈兄貴〉：気に入らないなら、他の店へ行きゃいい。: Aniki: “If they don't like it, they can go to another shop.”
〈彦麻呂〉：そーゆー馬鹿な事を言ってるから、かき入れ時のお昼にガラ空きなんです。: Hikomaro: “It is because you say such foolish things that this place is empty even during the peak lunch hour.”
〈兄貴〉：とっとと金を払って出て行けっ。テメーみたいな怪しいオッサンに、アレコレ: Aniki: “Pay your money and get the hell out. I don't have to listen to some suspicious old man tell me what to do!”
〈彦麻呂〉：こちらこそ余計な事を言ってすみません。お兄さんの態度が目に余ったもの: Hikomaro: “I am the one who should apologize for saying unnecessary things. Your brother’s attitude was just too much to bear, so I spoke up.”
〈彦麻呂〉：お値段はリーズナブルなのに、どうしてプライドだけは、あんなに高いんで: Hikomaro: “The prices are reasonable, so why is his pride so high-de ojaru ne?”
〈雪菜〉：たくさんのお客さんに食べて貰いたくて、お兄ちゃんは今の値段設定にしたん: Yukina: “Onii-chan set the current prices because he wanted many customers to eat. He wants various people to enjoy the taste he's proud of.”
〈彦麻呂〉：その考えが全うされてるなら、何も問題はないと思いますが。: Hikomaro: “If that thought were actually being fulfilled, I would think there is no problem, but...”
〈彦麻呂〉：現状、もう少し愛想のスパイスが必要かと、はい。: Hikomaro: “As it stands, I believe a little more spice in the form of friendliness is needed, yes.”
〈雪菜〉：…バイク屋さんの時からそうでした。: Yukina: “...It’s been like that since his motorcycle shop days.”
〈雪菜〉：ちょっとした事で、すぐカッとなって。カチンとくると感情を抑えられず、相: Yukina: “He gets heated over the smallest things. When something rubs him the wrong way, he can’t control his emotions, and he’ll snap at anyone.”
〈彦麻呂〉：…もしかして絶望してますかぁ？: Hikomaro: “...Are you perhaps in despair-de ojaru ka?”
〈雪菜〉：絶望って言うか、どうする事もできない自分が、もどかしいです。: Yukina: “Despair... or rather, it’s frustrating that I can't do anything about it.”
〈彦麻呂〉：ご本人が気づけば何とかなりますよ。: Hikomaro: “If he realizes it himself, something can be done-desuuuu.”
〈彦麻呂〉：人間は学習する生き物ですから。根本的な性格は変わりませんが、考え方は: Hikomaro: “Humans are creatures that learn. While a fundamental personality does not change, ways of thinking can change as much as one likes-desuuuu.”
〈彦麻呂〉：塩ラーメンと半チャーハン、美味しゅうございました。次にお邪魔した時も: Hikomaro: “The salt ramen and half-fried rice were delicious. The next time I visit, please do ask me how the taste was-desuuuu.”
〈彦麻呂〉：今日の担々麺と野菜炒め、美味しゅうございました。次にお邪魔した時も是: Hikomaro: “Today’s tantanmen and stir-fried vegetables were delicious. The next time I visit, please do ask me how the taste was-desuuuu.”
〈彦麻呂〉：タンメン大盛りにライス、美味しゅうございました。次にお邪魔した時も是: Hikomaro: “The large portion of tanmen and rice was delicious. The next time I visit, please do ask me how the taste was-desuuuu.”
〈兄貴〉：…マズいなら何で来るんだよ。: Aniki: “...If it’s bad, why the hell do you keep coming?”
〈彦麻呂〉：ですから味の話ではございません。: Hikomaro: “Like I said, I am not talking about the taste-desuuuu.”
〈兄貴〉：味以外の事だろうが、気に入らないなら、来るな。: Aniki: “Even if it's not the taste, if you don't like it, don't come.”
〈彦麻呂〉：どうしてお客さんの注文を無視したんですかぁ？: Hikomaro: “Why did you ignore that customer’s order-de ojaru ka?”
〈彦麻呂〉：私の横にいた方の話ですよぉ。まさか聞こえてなかったなんて、馬鹿な言い: Hikomaro: “The person who was sitting next to me-desuuuu. Surely you won't make the foolish excuse that you didn't hear them-de ojaru ne.”
〈兄貴〉：接客は妹の役目だ。: Aniki: “Customer service is my sister’s job.”
〈彦麻呂〉：妹さんは、他のお客さんの注文を、承ってる最中でしたぁ。: Hikomaro: “The sister was in the middle of taking another customer’s order-desuuuu.”
〈兄貴〉：俺が接客するとロクな事にならない。すぐケンカになったり、店の空気が悪く: Aniki: “Nothing good happens when I handle customers. We end up fighting, or the atmosphere in the shop turns sour.”
〈彦麻呂〉：今のままでも空気は悪いと思いますが。: Hikomaro: “I think the atmosphere is already sour as it is-desuuuu.”
〈兄貴〉：青アザを作らないだけでもマシだろ？: Aniki: “It’s better than leaving someone with a blue bruise, isn't it?”
〈彦麻呂〉：だからこれからも無視すると。: Hikomaro: “So you intend to keep ignoring them from now on-desuuuu.”

一枚ずつ口の中へ放り込む。: And stuffed it into his mouth one page at a time.
〈彦麻呂〉：やはりアナタは話のわかるお方ですねぇ。: Hikomaro: “As I thought, you are a reasonable person.”
〈彦麻呂〉：これで全てなかった事になりました。: Hikomaro: “With this, everything has been undone.”
〈横山〉：このっ、へめっ、ははへすむほ、おもふなよっ。: Yokoyama: “Y-You... don’t think you’ll get away with this!”
〈彦麻呂〉：ただで済むとは思ってません。: Hikomaro: “I don’t expect to just ‘get away’ with it.”
〈彦麻呂〉：今までロクな生き方をしてなかったせいか、失う物も大してございませんの: Hikomaro: “Perhaps because I haven’t lived much of a decent life, I don’t have much to lose... yes.”
〈横山〉：ほ、ほこほんほいこんでやるっ。: Yokoyama: “I-I’ll have you thrown in jail!”
〈彦麻呂〉：こちらも込み入るなら徹底的にいきますぅ。生き死にのレベルで争い、お互: Hikomaro: “If this is going to get complicated, I shall go all the way. A life-or-death struggle, and both of us scattering brilliantly at the end... that might be beautiful as well.”
〈彦麻呂〉：だいたいの状況は把握致しましたので。あとはこの方の解毒を致しまして、: Hikomaro: “I have grasped the general situation. Now, I shall administer the antidote to this gentleman and have an adult conversation with them.”
〈彦麻呂〉：生き死にレベルの争いは、話し合った後という事で。: Hikomaro: “The life-or-death struggle will happen after we talk.”
〈横山〉：は、はかったから、ひひはへん、ほのへほはなへっ。: Yokoyama: “I-I got it already, I’m not suing, so let me go!”
さっきまでの喧騒はどこへやら。: The previous commotion vanished into thin air.
全員が少しずつ冷静さを取り戻し。: Everyone regained a bit of their composure.
取り敢えずは仕切直しの雰囲気に。: An atmosphere of starting over, for the time being.
奥さんはキッチンに立ち。: The Ma’am stood in the kitchen.
横山と布施はソファーに座り。: Yokoyama and Fuse sat on the sofa.
旦那だけ相変わらず和室に正座で。: Only the husband remained sitting upright in the Japanese-style room as before.
みんな横山のロレツが戻るのを大人しく待ってる。: Everyone waited quietly for Yokoyama’s speech to return to normal.
もちろんその顔に笑みはなく。: Naturally, there was no smile on his face.
まずは麻呂の土下座から。: It began with Maro’s deep kowtow.
額をフローリングに擦りつけ。: Pressing my forehead against the flooring.
暴力を振るった事を心から詫びた。: I apologized from the bottom of my heart for resorting to violence.
横山からの嫌味はあったけど。: There were snide remarks from Yokoyama.
そこから取っ組み合いには至らない。: But it didn’t lead back to a physical scuffle.
残るは大人の話し合い。: What remained was an adult conversation.
それぞれが非のある部分を認め。: Each person admitted to their faults.
それぞれが妥協点を見出していく。: Each person found a point of compromise.
和室にいた旦那も今はリビングで。: The Husband, who had been in the Japanese-style room, was now in the living room.
事の発端である自分を総括させられてた。: He was made to reflect on himself as the source of the incident.
時間の経過と共に麻呂は中立となり。: As time passed, Maro became neutral.
いつの間にやら双方の言い分を整理する係に。: Before long, I was the one organizing both sides’ arguments.
〈横山〉：っ、この人が混ざった時点で、もうどうにもならんだろ。: Yokoyama: “Ugh, once this guy got involved, there was no stopping it, was there?”
どうにもならない対象は奥さんで。: The ‘it’ being the situation with the Ma’am.
何となく話の落とし処が見えてきてる。: A resolution was somehow coming into view.
〈横山〉：みんなが一筆書くって事でどう？: Yokoyama: “How about everyone signs a statement?”
早く散会させる為の提案を横山がしてくる。: He proposed a way to end the meeting quickly.
あとは誰が何を書くか。: All that remained was who would write what.
事態を収拾させる為の話し合いとなった。: The discussion turned to settling the situation.
まずキッカケを作った旦那。: First, the Husband, who provided the spark.
二度と邪な企ては起こさず。: He would never again hatch such a wicked scheme.
これからは良き伴侶として奥さんに尽くす。: From now on, he would devote himself to the Ma’am as a good partner.
麻呂の件も自己の責任とし。: He would take responsibility for the Maro incident.
治療費が発生した場合は全て負担する旨。: And in the event of medical expenses, he would bear them all.
今まで撮影したデータを旦那に渡し。: He would hand over all recorded data to the Husband.
今後は二度と綾瀬夫妻に関わらない。: From now on, he would never involve himself with the Ayases again.
どちらにしろ自分は離婚調停中で。: He was in the middle of divorce mediation anyway.
三ヶ月以内に引っ越しする事も書き加えてた。: He also added that he would move out within three months.
何も口外せず。: He would not breathe a word to anyone.
今後の付き合いは挨拶程度で。: Any future interaction would be limited to greetings.
変な誘惑にも二度と乗らず。: He would never succumb to strange temptations again.
時期を見て引っ越しも視野に入れる。: He would also consider moving out when the time was right.
彼女自身も書くと言ったけど。: She said she would write something as well.
明記する内容が誰も思い浮かばず。: But no one could think of anything specific for her to state.
結局は紙を渡されただけでオシマイ。: In the end, she was just handed a piece of paper.
それがよほど手持ち無沙汰だったのか。: Perhaps she felt awkward having nothing to do.
少し離れた場所に一人ポツンと座り。: She sat alone, a bit away from the others.
時間を埋めるよう何かカリカリ描いてた。: Doodling something as if to pass the time.
リビングは麻呂と奥さんと旦那だけに。: Only Maro, the Ma’am, and the Husband remained in the living room.
時計を見れば午前２時で。: Looking at the clock, it was 2:00 AM.
麻呂が来てから６時間近く経とうとしてた。: Nearly six hours had passed since Maro arrived.
その表情はちょっと泣きそう。: She looked like she was about to cry.
奥さんがよりハグしやすくなるよう。: And to make it easier for her.
〈彦麻呂〉：抱き締める準備はできておりますよぉ。: Hikomaro: “I am ready to embrace you!”
胸に飛び込んできたのは旦那。: The one who dove into my chest was the Husband.
ズルズルと麻呂の足へしがみつく感じに。: And he ended up clinging to Maro’s legs.
〈夫〉：っ、本当に本当にすみませんでしたぁ。: Husband: “I-I am truly, truly, truly sorry!”
麻呂と奥さんは目を合わせ。: Maro and the Ma’am made eye contact.
だんだんこれで良かったと思うように。: I began to feel that this was for the best.
奥さんとハグしてたら。: If I had hugged the Ma’am.
それはそれで妙でおじゃったね。: That would have been quite awkward, degozaru.
ここは綾瀬家のリビングで。: This is the Ayase living room.
出過ぎた真似は御法度でおじゃる。: Overstepping one’s bounds is strictly forbidden, degozaru.
〈彦麻呂〉：ヒマじゃなくても来ましたが。: Hikomaro: “I would have come even if I were not free.”
〈彦麻呂〉：取り敢えず無事で良かったです。: Hikomaro: “In any case, I am glad you are safe.”
〈咲美〉：…もうこんな時間だし、泊まっていかない？: Saki: “...It’s already this late; why don’t you stay the night?”
〈夫〉：ぼ、僕からも是非お願いしますぅ。: Husband: “P-Please, I beg of you too!”
〈彦麻呂〉：…お言葉に甘えましょうかねぇ。: Hikomaro: “...Then I shall take you up on that offer.”
マー君が謝っても奥さんは許さず。: Even if Ma-kun apologized, the Ma’am might not have forgiven him.
横山達がいた時以上に、空気が悪くなってたかも。: The atmosphere might have become even worse than when Yokoyama and the others were there.
嫌な事も全て想い出となってくれる事を。: And even the unpleasant things would all become memories.
尖ってた角が丸くなり。: The sharp edges would round out.
必ず笑って話せる時は来る。: A time when they could laugh and talk about it would surely come.
辛い時でも我慢できるのでおじゃるよ…。: I can endure even the painful times, degozaru...
